name: A Better Way Auto Brokerage
description_short: Chicago auto broker helping credit-challenged buyers source, price-negotiate, and finance new or used vehicles — specializing in bad credit, bankruptcy, and repossession cases.
description_long:
A Better Way Auto Brokerage was founded on January 30, 2016 by Reginald Rogers and operates out of Chicago's South Side at 2609 W. 63rd St. The company holds a BBB A+ rating and has been BBB-accredited since June 2018. It carries no federal certifications such as CDFI or HUD approval — it is a for-profit private business serving the Chicagoland metro area exclusively. The team claims over 20 years of combined industry experience in auto sales, financing, and credit-related services, and has built a consistent local presence over its first decade.

A Better Way functions as a true auto broker: they do not maintain inventory or sell cars off a lot. Instead, they source new and used vehicles on a client's behalf, pre-negotiate pricing with dealerships, and arrange auto financing. Their core market is customers who face difficulty qualifying through conventional channels — specifically those with bad credit, no credit history, recent repossessions, open or discharged bankruptcies, no driver's license, fixed incomes, self-employment income, or limited credit histories. Beyond the vehicle transaction itself, they also refer clients to credit restoration services and auto insurance providers as needed.

The company's public reputation is notably strong for a local brokerage: a 5.0/5 Google rating across 596 reviews is exceptional and suggests consistently positive customer experiences. Their value proposition centers on access — securing vehicle approvals and pricing that buyers "couldn't possibly get otherwise" through pre-negotiated dealer relationships. That focus on credit-challenged buyers in an underserved part of Chicago's South Side represents a community-oriented niche that larger dealerships and conventional banks routinely overlook.

For Chicagoland residents who have been turned away elsewhere, A Better Way fills a genuine gap in the market. Their strengths lie in personal service, local market knowledge, and a demonstrated willingness to work with nearly any credit profile. The honest limitations are worth noting: they are strictly local and serve no one outside the Chicago area; broker fees and financing rates are not publicly disclosed and must be obtained by contacting the company directly; and since they are a broker rather than a direct lender, the actual loan terms originate with third-party lenders and will vary based on each customer's individual credit profile. Prospective clients should ask upfront about all costs before engaging.
pros: Perfect 5.0/5 Google rating from 596 verified reviews — exceptional for a local finance-adjacent service
BBB A+ rated and accredited since June 2018
Works with virtually any credit situation: bad credit, no credit, bankruptcy, repossession, no driver's license
Acts as a buyer's agent — pre-negotiates vehicle pricing on the customer's behalf with dealerships
Over 20 years of combined industry experience per company marketing
Accepts fixed-income and self-employed borrowers often turned away by traditional lenders
Established 2016 with a decade-long track record serving Chicago's South Side community
cons: Strictly local — serves Chicagoland only, no remote or out-of-state customers
Broker fees and financing rates are not published publicly; must be requested directly
No online application, customer portal, or dedicated mobile app found
Closed Sundays; weekday hours end at 7pm, limiting accessibility for standard 9-to-5 workers
As a broker, actual loan terms come from third-party lenders — rates are not set or controlled by the brokerage
best_for: Chicago-area residents with bad credit, no credit, or recent bankruptcy or repossession who need vehicle financing
Fixed-income or self-employed individuals who struggle to qualify through traditional auto lenders
First-time buyers or credit newcomers who want guided, pre-negotiated vehicle purchasing support
Buyers who want a representative to negotiate pricing on their behalf rather than facing dealerships alone
diagnosis: A Better Way Auto Brokerage is best suited for Chicagoland residents who have been turned down by conventional dealerships or lenders due to damaged or limited credit — particularly those dealing with bankruptcy, repossession, or non-traditional income. Their 5.0 Google rating across nearly 600 reviews reflects genuine, consistent client satisfaction for a local operation. The main caveat is that they are a broker, not a lender: actual financing costs depend on third-party lenders and are not disclosed publicly, so buyers should confirm all fees and rates before committing.
services: New vehicle sourcing and purchasing assistance
Used vehicle sourcing and purchasing assistance
Price pre-negotiation with dealerships on buyer's behalf
Auto financing arrangement for bad credit borrowers
Full buyer representation throughout the vehicle purchase process
